text: Establish remote-persistent backlog artifact handling because .backlogit is gitignored and shipment/backlog state may not survive handoff
Stage the Document Ingestion and Schema Validation Pipeline design into full backlog decomposition, phased shipments, and CLI/MCP parity work
Fix pyright type-check regressions in process metadata and AST lint after shipment 004-S merge (metadata.py:80, ast_lint.py:11-14)

Align docline markdown output with graphtor-docs ingestion contract: shared frontmatter schema with description/content_sha256/forward-slash source_path/schema_version, JSON Schema export, H1/H2/H3 chunk-boundary heading validation, DOCX <w:pStyle> heading mapping + tables/lists, PDF layout-aware extraction via font-size histogram (and opt-in docling layout), HTML semantic zone preservation, sitemap discovery, URL canonicalization, staging metadata promoted to WebFrontmatter, optional chunk-stable anchors, cross-tool contract design doc, and E2E integration verification. Goal: markdown emitted by docline ingests directly into graphtor-docs CozoDB/HNSW without re-normalization. Full gap inventory and proposed shipment decomposition (F1-F8) in docs/scratch/2026-06-02-docline-graphtor-alignment-gap-analysis.md.
Restore fresh Copilot review requestability for docline repository. Source: docs/closure/001-S-backlog-artifact-persistence-prerequisite.md

[HARVESTED into 011-S → 011-F → 011.001-T on 2026-06-03] Add GitHub Actions CI workflow for docline (ruff format --check, ruff check, pyright src/, pytest, build). Run on Linux runners to dodge Windows tmp_path PermissionError noise (CE758832). Recommended in 010-S operational closure.

[HARVESTED into 011-S → 011-F → 011.002-T on 2026-06-03] Investigate pytest tmp PermissionError noise on Windows (176+ entries during pytest runs). Bundled into 011-S as a documentation-only CONTRIBUTING.md note. Deep root-cause investigation is deferred to stash 0AA8B223.

Update tests/parity/test_manifest_parity.py and tests/parity/test_equivalence.py to expect 3 tools (fetch, process, export_schema) instead of 2. Pre-existing stale tests from 010.005-T (JSON Schema export CLI added export_schema tool but parity tests not updated).

Parity tests test_manifest_has_two_tools and test_manifest_tool_names_match_operation_names hard-code 2-tool count but manifest now exposes 3 (fetch, process, export-schema). Update assertions to match current parity surface.
Support loading multiple configuration files, including configurations stored in directories such as .elt/config.

End-to-end validation for multi-source ingestion: use .elt as the extract-land-transform staging area (with best-effort directory naming) for PDFs and DOCX files in .elt plus website crawl and GitHub repository sources defined by YAML config files; thoroughly test and verify processing across all source types and ensure outputs normalize to schema-conformant markdown content standards.

[G3a] Replace page/char-based output splitting with heading-aware semantic segmentation. Current behavior (src/docline/process/output_contract.py): PDF splits per physical page via read_pdf_pages(); DOCX splits via _chunk_text_blocks(blocks, 6000)=greedy char bins. Both cut mid-section, breaking AST/Tree-sitter graphing. Proposed: new src/docline/process/segment.py that parses rendered markdown with markdown-it-py (already a dep) into an AST and splits at H1 boundaries (hard), sub-splits at H2 when a part exceeds max_chars (default 12k), and falls through to current char-bin behavior as the safety net only. Applies to both PDF (after extractor output) and DOCX (after read_docx_blocks). Web crawl already one-page-per-file — no change. Tests: add tests/process/test_segment.py covering H1-only, H1+H2, oversize H1, no-heading-fallback. Touches output_contract.py and one new module; no schema break (filenames still part-NNNN.md but content is semantically bounded). Source: operator-raised concern that current splits destroy chapter/section continuity needed for AST-based graphing.

[G3b] Add in-frontmatter referentiality + emit chunk anchors for AST/Tree-sitter graphing. Currently per-part .md files are structurally orphaned ΓÇö only manifest.json carries part order. _inject_chunk_anchors() exists in src/docline/process/assemble.py but emit_chunk_anchors=False by default and is never set True. Proposed additions under the BaseFrontmatter 'docline:' namespace (no v1 contract break ΓÇö additionalProperties: true): document_id (already in manifest), parent_document_id (same id for all parts of a source), part_index (1-based), total_parts, prev_part (relative path or null), next_part, section_title (H1 anchoring this part once G3a lands). Also: flip emit_chunk_anchors default to True in process pipeline so every .md ships chunk-NNNN anchors usable by graphtor's existing markdown_it/Tree-sitter parsers. Tests: tests/process/test_referentiality.py asserting part-0002.md has prev_part=part-0001.md, total_parts matches manifest count, anchor injection survives heading hierarchy. Re-export schema after change; update snapshot at d:/Source/GitHub/graphtor-docs/schemas/docline/base-frontmatter-v1.schema.json. Source: operator-raised concern that individual part files lack referentiality to enable graph-builder reconstruction.

[G3c] Wire docling as PDF engine + sidecar image extraction for DOCX/PDF to preserve tables, figures, code blocks, and layout structure in emitted markdown. Current behavior: pypdf returns flat text only (0 tables, 0 figures from ~50 .md from real PDFs); DOCX has _render_table() and emits GFM tables when <w:tbl> present, but 31 <w:drawing> elements per source DOCX produce 0 image references. Proposed (4 sub-pieces): (1) Add [project.optional-dependencies] pdf=['docling>=2,<3'] to pyproject.toml (no current optional-deps section ΓÇö establishing the pattern). (2) Default read_pdf_pages(layout_engine='auto') ΓÇö resolve to 'docling' when dependencies.pdf_available() else 'heuristic'. Expose CLI flag 'docline process --pdf-engine {auto,docling,heuristic}' and YAML config 'pdf_engine: auto' per source. _read_pdf_docling_pages already exists in src/docline/readers/pdf.py:471 ΓÇö just needs wiring. (3) DOCX image sidecar: walk <w:drawing>/<a:blip r:embed>, resolve via word/_rels/document.xml.rels, extract word/media/imageN.png to {output_root}/{job_id}/media/figure-NNNN.png, emit ![alt](media/figure-NNNN.png) at source position. (4) PDF image sidecar: configure docling PdfPipelineOptions(images_scale>0) and thread output through to same media/ dir. Manifest gains media_files: [...]. RISK: docline venv is Python 3.14.0; docling 2.97.0 supports >=3.10 but its transitive ML deps (torch, onnxruntime) may lack 3.14 wheels (smoke install hung silently for >5min, likely source-building). Plan must include: probe ecosystem compatibility, optionally pin pdf-extra to Python <3.14 in pyproject classifier, or document waiting for upstream wheels. Tests: tests/fixtures/sample-with-tables.docx (real <w:tbl>), sample-with-images.docx (<w:drawing>+embedded PNG), sample-multi-heading.pdf for docling layout. Source: operator-raised hard requirement that markdown formatting (tables, figures, lists) survive into output to enable AST/Tree-sitter graphing.

[P-009 compliance] Repo setting `allow_rebase_merge: true` violates strict P-009 (constitution Principle XI: merge commit history preservation). Discovered during 012-S Ship Step 5.14 pre-merge guardrail check. Action: GitHub → Settings → General → Pull Requests → uncheck "Allow rebase merging". `allow_squash_merge: false` and `allow_merge_commit: true` are already correct. No code change required — repo-admin setting only.

[Cross-repo follow-up from 013-S] Refresh graphtor-docs schema snapshot for the new BaseFrontmatter docline namespace referentiality keys (`parent_document_id`, `part_index`, `total_parts`, `prev_part`, `next_part`, `section_title`). Steps: (1) from docline workspace run `python -m docline export-schema > base-frontmatter-v1.schema.json`; (2) copy the file to `d:/Source/GitHub/graphtor-docs/schemas/docline/base-frontmatter-v1.schema.json`; (3) open a separate PR in the graphtor-docs repo. This is forbidden inside the docline workspace per Constitution P-IV (CLI Workspace Containment) and must be performed by the operator. Closure: docs/closure/013-S-referentiality.md.

[Hardening] `src/docline/process/segment.py:_char_bin` splits paragraphs on literal `"

"` only — would not handle CRLF-separated paragraphs (`"

"`). Surfaced as review F2 advisory during 012-S Ship Step 4.4 review. Current `read_pdf` and `read_docx_blocks` emit `
` so risk is low; add normalization on segmenter entry (`text.replace("
", "
")`) to harden against future extractor changes (e.g. when G3c lands docling, which may emit different line endings on Windows). Two-line change + one regression test.

[Docling tuning, plan-review F3 from 014-S] Enable `PdfPipelineOptions(do_table_structure=True, table_structure_options=TableStructureOptions(do_cell_matching=True))` and other docling tuning knobs in `_read_pdf_docling_pages` to maximize table-and-list fidelity in produced markdown. Lower priority — defaults are already a strict improvement over the heuristic baseline. Also wire `PdfPipelineOptions(generate_picture_images=True, images_scale=2.0)` to route docling-rendered pictures through the existing `PictureSink` (PictureSink plumbing landed in 014-S, but no docling caller exists yet). Closure: docs/closure/014-S-docling-sidecars.md.

Investigate root cause of Windows `tmp_path` `PermissionError` teardown noise (176+ entries per pytest run). Deeper than the local-dev workaround documented in CONTRIBUTING.md from 011-S. Blocks the cross-OS CI matrix follow-up.
Add cross-OS CI matrix (Windows + macOS) once Windows `tmp_path` PermissionError root cause is identified and fixed. Builds on 011-S (Linux-only CI).

[Spike + plan] Generate missing H1 headers to enable proper AST tree parsing and graph stitching for graphtor. Problem statement: load test on 2026-06-04 surfaced two failure modes both rooted in missing/disordered H1: (a) PartTableAndIndexStrat.docx opens with H2/H3 before any H1, which the strict graphtor-docs chunk-boundary parentage check rejects unless `--allow-heading-disorder` is passed (frontmatter assembly emits "H2 heading 'X' appeared before any H1" and refuses to emit the part); (b) the cosmos PDF heuristic output frequently has `section_title: null` for parts that contain only body text without an extractable heading, leaving graphtor without a parent anchor for those chunks. Spike scope: (1) characterize the population of "headerless" parts across the load test corpus (count parts with `section_title: null` per source, count DOCX/PDF/HTML sources that open without H1); (2) survey approaches: (a) deterministic — synthesize H1 from `title` frontmatter when no H1 found in body; (b) deterministic — promote first H2 to H1 when document has H2 but no H1 (the current `--allow-heading-disorder` flag silences the warning but doesn't fix the tree); (c) heuristic — first non-empty paragraph as H1 fallback; (d) SLM-based — invoke a small local language model to summarize the first N tokens of the part into an H1 (e.g., Phi-3.5-mini, gemma-2-2b, or similar; must run CPU-only on contributor laptops since we don't want to introduce GPU dependencies for ingestion); (e) hybrid — try deterministic first, escalate to SLM only when deterministic fails; (3) evaluate each on the headerless corpus and recommend an approach with trade-offs (determinism vs quality vs runtime cost vs new dependency surface); (4) propose where in the pipeline the synthesizer runs — at read time (in readers/), at assembly time (in process/assemble.py), or as a separate normalization pass. Output: docs/decisions/{date}-spike-h1-header-synthesis.md with corpus stats, approach comparison, recommendation, and follow-on shipment outline. Linked context: docs/closure/012-S-heading-aware-segmentation.md (H1/H2 splitter), docs/closure/013-S-referentiality.md (chunk anchors), docs/closure/015-S-post-g3-hygiene.md, and the load test artifacts at .elt/output/ from 2026-06-04.

[Task, P0] New module src/docline/runtime/resource_probe.py exposing probe() -> ResourceBudget. Single source of truth for ALL runtime throttling decisions (size gate, splitter trigger, docling concurrency, thread caps, GPU acceleration). Reads psutil.virtual_memory().available, os.cpu_count(), psutil.swap_memory(), and (when torch is importable) torch.cuda introspection at call time and returns a frozen dataclass with: available_ram_gb, total_ram_gb, logical_cpus, pagefile_pressure (used/total > 0.5), accelerator_device (one of "cpu", "cuda", "mps"), gpu_name (str | None), gpu_vram_gb (float, free VRAM), gpu_compute_capability (tuple[int,int] | None), recommended_concurrency (1 when constrained; up to cpu//2 when ample; higher when GPU), recommended_docling_max_pages (CPU: 0/25/50/75/100 stepped by RAM tier; GPU: 2-4× the CPU value for the same RAM tier since GPU has its own memory pool), recommended_docling_max_mb (0/10/20/30/40 CPU, scaled for GPU), serialize_docling (True when only one docling worker can fit safely; almost always False on GPU since CUDA OOMError is cleanly catchable per-call).

GPU detection logic (all conditions required to set accelerator_device="cuda"):
1. torch is importable AND torch.cuda.is_available() is True
2. torch.cuda.get_device_capability(0) >= (5, 0) — Maxwell or newer. Kepler (sm_3x) is rejected because PyTorch 2.x ships no kernels for it (the 2026-06-04 host machine's GTX 770M is sm_30 and fails this check). Optionally allow operator override via DOCLINE_GPU_FORCE=1 for testing.
3. torch.cuda.mem_get_info(0)[0] >= 4 * 1024**3 — at least 4 GB FREE VRAM. docling rt_detr-l4 working set is ~4-6 GB; below 4 GB free we OOM immediately.
4. Driver is recent enough that the installed torch CUDA build can launch kernels (probe by trying a 1-element torch.zeros(1, device='cuda'); on failure, log and downgrade to CPU).

GPU policy when accelerator_device="cuda":
- Pass AcceleratorOptions(device=AcceleratorDevice.CUDA) through to docling in _read_pdf_docling_pages (src/docline/readers/pdf.py)
- recommended_docling_max_pages = min(200, gpu_vram_gb * 25) — GPU memory allocator is hard-capped and CUDA OOMError is per-call-recoverable, so larger chunks are safe
- serialize_docling = False (CUDA failures are catchable and don't pollute the parent process)
- recommended_concurrency stays low (1-2) because torch CUDA contexts don't multi-tenant cleanly inside one process; rely on the subprocess-per-chunk isolation from stash D885CE79 for parallelism if needed
- Thread caps (stash C1EB2C6A) still apply for the host-side preprocessing (rasterization, tokenization)

CPU policy when accelerator_device="cpu" (the default on the 2026-06-04 host and most contributor hardware):
- Threshold table (unchanged from prior stash revision):
  | available_ram_gb | concurrency | docling_max_pages | docling_max_mb | serialize_docling |
  | < 4              | 1           | 0 (heuristic only) | 0              | n/a               |
  | 4 – 8            | 1           | 25                 | 10             | True              |
  | 8 – 16           | 1           | 50                 | 20             | True              |
  | 16 – 32          | 2           | 75                 | 30             | False             |
  | > 32             | min(cpu//2,4)| 100               | 40             | False             |

Pagefile pressure detection: when psutil.swap_memory().percent > 50, force serialize_docling=True and halve recommended_docling_max_pages regardless of nominal free RAM. Catches the 2026-06-04 case where free RAM looked OK but the pager was already thrashing. Applies in BOTH cpu and cuda modes (host RAM still matters for rasterization buffers even when the model lives on GPU).

The probe is cheap (~milliseconds; one psutil call + one torch.cuda call if CUDA is available) and is re-run once per batch run. Result is logged at INFO level so every run captures the budget that drove its decisions — that log line is the first thing you grep for when triaging a future load-test issue.

Tests: parameterized RED tests covering:
- each CPU RAM tier (5 cases)
- pagefile-pressure override (force psutil to report > 50% swap)
- the always-serial path on <8GB systems
- GPU available but compute capability < (5, 0) → falls back to cpu (Kepler rejection)
- GPU available but free VRAM < 4 GB → falls back to cpu
- GPU available, capability OK, VRAM OK → returns accelerator_device="cuda" with raised page limits
- torch not importable → returns accelerator_device="cpu" cleanly (no ImportError leak)
- DOCLINE_GPU_FORCE=1 override path

Mock torch.cuda calls in tests so the suite runs on CPU-only contributor machines.

Gates everything else in shipment A (4B913619, 15ADD215, C1EB2C6A). On the 2026-06-04 host (i7-4700MQ + 32 GB RAM + GTX 770M sm_30 3 GB VRAM) the probe deterministically returns accelerator_device="cpu" because the GPU fails the compute-capability gate; the GPU detection code path is dormant but tested. On a contributor's RTX 3060 (8 GB VRAM, sm_86) it would return accelerator_device="cuda" with recommended_docling_max_pages=200, transparently delivering 3-10× speedups without any code change in the splitter or batch processor.

See docs/memory/2026-06-05/rca-2026-06-04-load-test-system-oom.md remediation 1 and the 2026-06-05 GPU addendum.

[Spike, P0, refined by 2026-06-05 RCA] Identify maximum safe PDF size / page count for docling layout model on the target hardware tiers. Load test on 2026-06-04 crashed docling rt_detr layout model with `RuntimeError: [enforce fail at alloc_cpu.cpp:117] DefaultCPUAllocator: not enough memory: you tried to allocate 6553600 bytes` while processing azure-cosmos-db.pdf (~554-part output), and the system entered a paging spiral that required hard reboot. The `--pdf-engine auto` fallback only catches `PdfReadError`, NOT `RuntimeError`/`MemoryError`, so the entire batch aborted with no output emitted. **Design pivot from the RCA**: the size gate now feeds the PDF SPLITTER (F64683BC), not the heuristic engine. Heuristic is only the last-resort fallback when the splitter itself can't run. Spike scope: (a) characterize docling rt_detr peak working-set memory growth as a function of page count, total document bytes, and image-richness, on representative target hardware tiers (8GB, 16GB, 32GB, >32GB); (b) determine the conservative thresholds that feed resource_probe.probe()'s recommended_docling_max_pages and recommended_docling_max_mb (stash 1D945AB5) for each tier; (c) the broader exception net (stash 15ADD215) covers the safety-net side; (d) measure whether subprocess isolation per chunk (stash D885CE79 remediation 6) reliably contains c10::Error failures. Unblocks splitter (F64683BC) and batch processor (D885CE79) by providing the threshold table. Spike output: docs/decisions/{date}-spike-docling-pdf-size-threshold.md with measured numbers per hardware tier and the recommended ResourceBudget table. Linked: docs/memory/2026-06-05/rca-2026-06-04-load-test-system-oom.md, docs/closure/015-S-post-g3-hygiene.md (engine resolution rules), docs/closure/014-S-docling-sidecars.md (auto fallback in read_pdf_pages).

[Bug fix, P0] Broaden read_pdf_pages auto-fallback exception net in src/docline/readers/pdf.py:512. Currently only catches (PdfReadError, FileNotFoundError); must also catch RuntimeError, MemoryError, and OSError so docling rt_detr CPU allocator failures (the 2026-06-04 cosmos-db.pdf OOM that aborted the entire batch) fall back to heuristic instead of propagating out and stopping the run. Log at WARNING with PDF path and underlying exception class name. Pairs with stash 4B913619 (size pre-flight gate); these two together close the "single hostile PDF kills the batch" failure mode. Acceptance: unit test with a doubled _read_pdf_docling_pages that raises RuntimeError must complete with engine downgrade to heuristic; existing PdfReadError test must still pass. See docs/memory/2026-06-05/rca-2026-06-04-load-test-system-oom.md remediation 2.

[Task, refined by 2026-06-05 RCA] Cap docling/PyTorch thread fan-out in _read_pdf_docling_pages. Before the first docling import in src/docline/readers/pdf.py, derive the thread count from resource_probe.probe() (stash 1D945AB5) rather than hardcoding: `threads = str(max(1, budget.logical_cpus // 4))` then `os.environ.setdefault("OMP_NUM_THREADS", threads)`, same for MKL_NUM_THREADS, OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS, and TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM=false. PyTorch reads these at import time, so the setdefault MUST happen before the import block at line 564-572. setdefault (not setenv) respects operator overrides. On the 2026-06-04 hardware (i7-4700MQ, 8 logical cores) this resolves to threads=2; on a 16-core workstation it would resolve to 4. Reduces docling rolling working-set by avoiding per-thread tensor copies. Companion to stash 4B913619 (size gate → splitter) and 15ADD215 (broader exception net) in shipment A. See docs/memory/2026-06-05/rca-2026-06-04-load-test-system-oom.md remediation 5.

[Bug, P1, found during 2026-06-05 017-S closure] tests/parity/test_manifest_parity.py::test_cli_process_no_staging_dir_returns_1 is not actually CWD-isolated. It invokes main(["process"]) without setting a temp working directory, so when the developer's CWD contains leftover `.elt/output/` or repo-root `output/` from a prior `docline process` run, the test discovers those as staging dirs and recursively invokes docling against the real PDFs found there. On the 2026-06-04 host this caused pytest to hang for 30+ minutes on a single test that was supposed to take milliseconds. The expected exit code (1) was never reached because the CLI proceeded into actual processing. Fix: wrap the test in tmp_path or monkeypatch.chdir so the CLI sees no staging dir regardless of dev machine state. Apply same pattern to neighboring tests that depend on no-staging behavior. Audit other pytest cases that invoke main() without CWD isolation. Reference: docs/memory/2026-06-05/rca-2026-06-04-load-test-system-oom.md (this bug surfaced while clearing pytest baseline for the 017-S closure PR).

[Feature, P0, promoted from P1 by 2026-06-05 design pivot] PDF splitter for oversized PDFs that exceed the resource probe's per-call docling limits. Now the DEFAULT path for any PDF beyond the probe threshold, NOT just a fallback. Module placement: src/docline/readers/pdf_splitter.py. API: `split_pdf(path: Path, max_pages: int, page_overlap: int = 2) -> list[Path]` returning paths to a deterministic, hashed sub-PDF set staged under a temp/cache directory. max_pages is supplied by resource_probe.probe().recommended_docling_max_pages (stash 1D945AB5), NOT hardcoded. Requirements: (a) lossless page extraction via pypdf (already in dependency graph; pikepdf fallback only if pypdf can't round-trip embedded forms); (b) split unit is page count, not byte count — docling memory pressure scales with page count × image density; (c) deterministic chunk identifiers `{source_hash}-chunk-NNNN.pdf` so re-runs reuse cached splits; (d) preserve original PDF metadata in each chunk for downstream provenance; (e) page_overlap default 2 enables header-group reconstruction in the batch processor (stash D885CE79); (f) emit a manifest sidecar mapping chunk index → original page range so the stitcher can map outputs back to original coordinates. Cosmos-db.pdf (109 MB, ~700 pages) under recommended_docling_max_pages=50 splits into ~14 chunks; on a 32-GB host with serialize_docling=True they process serially, bounding total peak RSS to single-chunk consumption. Tests: unit tests on a fixture corpus (small splittable PDF, large PDF with embedded forms, image-heavy PDF, single-page edge case). No CLI surface yet — invoked by the batch processor (D885CE79). Wires into: src/docline/elt/execute.py (post-fetch, pre-process), src/docline/readers/pdf.py (skip docling for already-split chunks since they're under threshold). Closure: docs/closure/{shipment}-pdf-splitter.md. Related: rca docs/memory/2026-06-05/rca-2026-06-04-load-test-system-oom.md remediation 3.

[Feature, P0, promoted from P1 by 2026-06-05 design pivot] Batch processor for split PDFs with subprocess isolation, adaptive throttling, page overlap, and post-split graph stitching for graphtor ingestion. Module placement: src/docline/process/pdf_batch.py. Workflow: (1) call resource_probe.probe() (stash 1D945AB5) to determine concurrency and serialization policy; (2) detect when a source PDF exceeds probe.recommended_docling_max_pages or recommended_docling_max_mb; (3) invoke the splitter (stash F64683BC) with probe.recommended_docling_max_pages and page_overlap=2; (4) process each chunk through docling IN A SUBPROCESS via `subprocess.run([sys.executable, "-m", "docline._tools.docling_worker", chunk_path, out_path])` so a c10::Error / SIGABRT in one chunk reaps cleanly without taking down the parent; (5) when probe.serialize_docling=True, process chunks one at a time with a 10-30s reclaim pause between calls (gives the OS time to actually return torch tensor pages to the free list — PyTorch's CPU allocator does not return memory reliably even after GC); when False, use ProcessPoolExecutor with max_workers=probe.recommended_concurrency; (6) reconcile per-chunk outputs by detecting overlapping header groups (H1/H2 anchors that appear in both chunk N's tail and chunk N+1's head) and deduplicating; (7) emit a single logical document output with continuous part-NNNN.md numbering AND a `docline:` key `split_chunks` recording the original chunk boundaries; (8) failure-of-one-chunk policy: if chunk K fails OR exits non-zero from subprocess, downgrade THAT chunk to heuristic, do not abort the batch (inverse of the 2026-06-04 cosmos load-test failure). Requirements: chunk boundary detection must be heading-anchor-aware (existing chunk-anchor IDs from 013-S referentiality); overlap reconciliation must be deterministic — if the same H1 appears in two overlapping chunks, the SECOND occurrence is dropped, the first's frontmatter wins; media files from each chunk remapped to unified document's media/ with chunk-prefix (e.g. `media/c0001-figure-0001.png`); unified manifest entry replaces per-chunk entries — graphtor consumes one logical document, not N chunks. Tests: integration test on a synthetic large PDF that exercises overlap reconciliation, chunk-level fallback, and the serial-vs-concurrent paths. Closure: docs/closure/{shipment}-pdf-batch-stitching.md. Related: rca docs/memory/2026-06-05/rca-2026-06-04-load-test-system-oom.md remediations 3 + 6.

[Task, refined by 2026-06-05 RCA] Build scripts/load_test.py harness for full-corpus PDF load tests. Workflow per PDF: (1) call resource_probe.probe() once (stash 1D945AB5), log the ResourceBudget so the run is reproducible; (2) if PDF size or page count exceeds probe limits, call the splitter (stash F64683BC) to produce N chunks under `.elt/splits/{source_hash}/chunk-NNNN.pdf`; (3) for each PDF (or chunk), fork a child via subprocess.run([sys.executable, "-m", "docline", "process", ...]) so child OOM (docling rt_detr CPU allocator failure on hostile PDFs like azure-cosmos-db.pdf 109MB) reaps cleanly without taking down the harness; (4) wrap each child with psutil to sample peak RSS, elapsed time, exit code; (5) when probe.serialize_docling=True, hold to one docling child at a time with 10-30s reclaim pauses between calls; otherwise allow up to probe.recommended_concurrency concurrent children; (6) emit TSV row per child: {file, chunk, mb, pages, engine, exit, elapsed_s, peak_rss_mb, output_parts, fallback_reason, probe_available_gb, probe_concurrency, probe_serialize}; (7) on child non-zero exit, retry the same chunk with explicit --pdf-engine heuristic and record both rows; (8) after all chunks process, invoke the stitcher (stash D885CE79) to produce one logical document output. Runs in three tiers: small (≤10MB), medium (10-30MB), large (>30MB). Sets OMP_NUM_THREADS / MKL_NUM_THREADS / OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS to budget.logical_cpus//4 in the child env. Output TSV is BOTH the regression baseline AND the empirical evidence that feeds the threshold spike (4B913619). **Operator constraint**: must be run from a plain PowerShell session, NEVER inside an agent tool call — the Copilot CLI co-hosted with docling triggered the 2026-06-04 paging spiral / hard reboot. Depends on shipment A (probe, gate, exception net, thread caps) AND shipment B (splitter, batch, stitch). See docs/memory/2026-06-05/rca-2026-06-04-load-test-system-oom.md Phase 1 of recommended approach.

Triage-then-repair hybrid PDF pipeline: run heuristic engine on whole PDF for a fast baseline, score each page with deterministic signals (char density, non-ASCII ratio, long-line/no-whitespace anomalies, multi-column whitespace gaps, table-char density, pypdf image/form-field metadata, missing-heading inference), coalesce flagged page indices into ranges with ±1 context buffer, splice those ranges into temp PDFs via pypdf.PdfWriter, run the existing docling_worker subprocess on each splice, then merge per-page outputs into a final list where flagged pages come from docling and the rest from heuristic. Estimated wall-clock on azure-cosmos-db.pdf (3,426 pages): ~70–90 min vs current ~9.5 h all-docling — a 6–8× speedup at equivalent fidelity on pages that need it. New modules: src/docline/process/fidelity_scorer.py (pure scoring functions), src/docline/process/page_range.py (coalescer), src/docline/process/pdf_triage.py (5-pass orchestrator mirroring pdf_batch.py shape). New CLI flag --pdf-mode triage opt-in; default behavior unchanged. Output contract should record per-page engine attribution as a downstream confidence signal. Risks: false-negative undetected mangling (mitigate with a --report-only validation mode and an optional --sample-rate QA tripwire that re-runs ~5% of "clean" pages through docling); false-positive over-flagging (low harm, only wastes ML cycles). Builds on existing infrastructure: read_pdf_pages(layout_engine="heuristic") already returns list[str] per page, split_pdf already chunks by page range, docling_worker already runs subprocess-per-input. Motivating evidence: docs/memory/2026-06-05/rca-2026-06-04-load-test-system-oom.md and the 2026-06-06 cosmos load-test that produced 12 chunks of 75 pages over 2h 25m, ETA ~9.5h total. POC scorer with 7 signal functions and coalescer lives at docs/scratch/2026-06-06-fidelity-scorer-poc.py (passes synthetic discrimination test). Acceptance criteria / calibration tasks: (1) implement --report-only validation mode that scores a PDF and emits a per-page TSV (page_index, signals, aggregate, needs_docling, reason) without running docling — run against the 12 completed cosmos chunks where docling output already exists, compare flag pattern to where docling actually produced richer markdown than heuristic would, validate signal thresholds before locking them; (2) implement --sample-rate FLOAT QA tripwire that randomly re-runs that fraction of "clean" (unflagged) pages through docling and diffs the output — catches false-negative undetected mangling; emit a tripwire-disagreement count in the run summary so regressions surface as a metric; (3) calibrate _SIGNAL_WEIGHTS in fidelity_scorer.py against the cosmos chunks corpus (where heuristic and docling output exist for the same pages) — replace hard-coded guess weights with empirically-derived values, ideally via a small offline calibration script that produces a JSON weights file the scorer loads. Promotion path per Constitution II: Stage harvest of this stash → harness-architect produces failing tests/process/test_fidelity_scorer.py + test_pdf_triage.py → build-feature lifts POC into src/docline/process/.

[follow-on from 021-S, complement to 60E6157D + 1380BD85] Replace pypdf.extract_text() with markitdown as the heuristic baseline in the triage pipeline. Empirical comparison on 5 cosmos pages (2026-06-07 bench in logs/markitdown-bench/): markitdown produces real markdown (numbered lists, code fences, headings) where pypdf produces flat text. For page 127 instructions: pypdf gives 'To enable this feature... 1. Go to...' (list inlined) while markitdown produces a proper markdown numbered list. For JSON/code/plain prose pages: roughly equivalent output. For tables (page 470): markitdown does NOT solve the problem (its pdfminer.six backend has the same limitation as pypdf — broken vertical text with 0 pipes). Per-page cost: ~250ms (acceptable for whole-doc baseline — ~14 min on cosmos vs ~5 min for pypdf, but produces materially richer markdown). Files: src/docline/process/pdf_triage.py:process_pdf_triaged (replace reader.pages[i].extract_text() with markitdown call), src/docline/process/output_contract.py:build_output_document_parts (auto-mode path could also benefit — consider plumbing), tests/process/test_pdf_triage.py (add fixture verifying markdown output > pypdf raw text), pyproject.toml (add markitdown[pdf] as a required dep — already installs cleanly into the venv). Should land WITH stashes 60E6157D (diff metric fix) and 1380BD85 (layout-complexity signal) so the next PA3/PA4 re-run validates the full system: better baseline + better tripwire + better scorer. Evidence: logs/markitdown-bench/page*-markitdown.md vs logs/markitdown-bench/page*-pypdf.txt.

[PA4 calibration blocker from 021-S] Improve the QA tripwire disagreement metric in pdf_triage._normalize_markdown(). The current implementation only strips trailing whitespace and collapses blank lines, which inflates the disagreement count by treating structurally-similar text (code fences wrapping the same content, different reading-order starting points, trivial whitespace/spacing variation) as full disagreements. Empirical evidence from PA4 cosmos run (2026-06-07): 32 of 33 sampled "clean" pages flagged as disagreements; manual inspection of 5 of those showed 4 were false positives (formatting/order differences with substantially the same content) and 1 was a genuine scorer miss (page 470 — a real markdown table that heuristic rendered as broken vertical text). Recommended fix: switch to lowercased + punctuation-stripped + tokenized Jaccard similarity (or normalized Levenshtein) with a configurable threshold (default ~0.7). Count as disagreement only when similarity is below the threshold. Without this fix, PA4 calibration cannot use qa_disagreements as a meaningful signal. Files: src/docline/process/pdf_triage.py:_normalize_markdown (replace), tests/process/test_pdf_triage_tripwire.py (add similarity-threshold scenarios), docs/closure/021-S-triage-then-repair.md (update calibration gate). Evidence: .elt/output/cosmos-tripwire/pa3-summary.json, .elt/output/cosmos-tripwire/splices/qa-*.md.

[PA4 calibration blocker from 021-S] Add a PDF-layout-structure signal to fidelity_scorer to detect mangled tables in heuristic output. The current scorer signals (char_density, non_ascii_ratio, long_unbroken_line, column_gap, table_char_density, image_heavy, form_fields) all score the HEURISTIC OUTPUT TEXT. They cannot detect the dominant scorer-miss failure mode: a real table in the source PDF that pypdf.extract_text() flattens into a single column of single words that looks like ordinary paragraph text. Empirical evidence: page 470 of azure-cosmos-db.pdf (Azure RBAC permissions table) rendered by heuristic as 'Action Description\nnnections/write connection\nMicrosoft.DBforMariaDB/...' (broken vertical text); docling renders it as a proper markdown table (4519 chars vs 2109 chars; 2.1x richer output). None of the existing signals fire on this. Recommended fix: add a signal that inspects the SOURCE pypdf.PageObject for structural cues: text-run X-coordinate clustering, column count via x-quantization, line density vs page area, presence of grid-like positioning. The signal looks at the PDF page object (not the extracted text) and fires when the heuristic-extracted text is suspiciously sparse relative to the layout complexity. Files: src/docline/process/fidelity_scorer.py (add new signal function + add to _SIGNAL_NAMES tuple + add default weight + integrate in score_page), tests/process/test_fidelity_scorer.py (add table-mangling test case with a fixture PDF). Recommended signal name: signal_layout_complexity. Once landed, re-run PA3+PA4 to verify page 470 (and similar table pages) now flag correctly.

Add --keep-page-pdfs flag to triage orchestrator to preserve baseline-NNNN.pdf artifacts after run completes. Currently these per-page PDFs (3,426 for cosmos) are valuable diagnostic artifacts for calibration studies but get deleted at run end. Flag default: false (current behavior). When set: preserve baseline-NNNN.pdf and splice-AAAA-BBBB.pdf files for offline analysis. See docs/decisions/2026-06-08-extraction-strategy-study.md.

023-F robustness: _parse_md_frontmatter in src/docline/process/output_contract.py fails on YAML frontmatter with mixed indentation (some keys prefixed with single leading space, others not). yaml.safe_load raises YAMLError, returns None, --- fences stay in body, heading extractor misinterprets second --- as setext H2 marker. Affects 8 of 16 frontmatter assembly failures in Power BI corpus run (e.g. includes/copilot-notes.md, includes/yes-paginated.md, includes/yes-report-server.md). Microsoft Learn build pipeline accepts these files; docline should too. Fix: lenient pre-processor that strips uniform leading whitespace before yaml.safe_load, OR regex-based key:value fallback when YAML parse fails. See docs/decisions/2026-06-09-powerbi-corpus-coverage.md Category B failure analysis.

023-F slice: implement `docline fetch local-dir <path>` source type. Walks a local docs repo directory, auto-discovers TOC.yml files for ingest ordering, emits crawl-manifest.json with "pages" key. Replaces ad-hoc scripts/study/stage_powerbi_full.py harness with proper fetch surface. UNBLOCKER for routine end-to-end docline fetch -> docline process against any cloned Microsoft Learn docs repo (powerbi-docs, fabric-docs, dax-docs, analysis-services-docs). Validated need: ran 1340-file Power BI corpus through process pipeline in 142s via staging harness in docs/decisions/2026-06-09-powerbi-corpus-coverage.md.

Add source-markdown ingestion pathway to docline. When source MD is available (Microsoft Learn, AWS Docs, K8s, React, Python, OSS libraries with public docs/ trees), fetch from GitHub source repo and normalize, bypassing PDF extraction entirely. Empirical: PostgreSQL source MD beats docling by 34% on AST structural density at 100x lower cost. Components: URL-to-repo mapper, DocFx adapter, source-MD normalizer, --source-mode CLI flag, manifest provenance fields. Extends roadmap as new 026-S shipment before 024-S scoring inversion (which has reduced priority once source-available corpora skip extraction). Evidence: .elt/output/source-md-spike/postgresql-source-md-metrics.json. Decision: docs/decisions/2026-06-08-source-md-ingestion-extension.md

[review follow-up from 019-F] Extract shared Pass 1-2 helper between process_pdf_triaged and triage_report_only in pdf_triage.py. Both functions currently duplicate the heuristic-extraction loop (~12 lines each) and the per-page scoring loop. Refactoring opportunity: introduce a private _heuristic_pass(path, reader) -> list[str] and _score_pass(heuristic_pages, reader, scorer) -> list[PageScore]. Reduces duplication and centralizes the per-page extraction error handling. Defer until a third caller appears or until a bug is found in one path but not the other.

FOLLOW-UP from 027-S T3 finding: auto-apply heading-disorder tolerance for include-fragment files. After T3 stripped YAML-frontmatter noise, 8 Category-C-equivalent files were revealed: include-fragment files whose body starts with H2/H3 because they're designed to be embedded under a parent doc's H1. Files: includes/core-visuals/tablix-columns-resize.md, includes/direct-query-sso.md, includes/refresh-datasources.md, includes/rls-desktop-define-roles.md, includes/rls-desktop-view-as-roles.md, developer/visuals/dynamic-drill-down.md (tabbed), developer/visuals/highlight.md (tabbed), monitor-power-bi-embedded.md, etc. Two approach options: (a) auto-detect files under includes/ subtree and apply allow_heading_disorder per-file; (b) detect "no H1 anywhere in body" as a separate category in the assembler and relax the rule for those. Either eliminates the remaining 16 Power BI strict-mode failures. Currently operator workaround: pass --allow-heading-disorder to docline ingest local-dir (proven to achieve 100% success rate). See 027-S closure doc "T3 empirical finding" section.

023-F slice: DocFx tabbed content handler. Recognize ### [Label](#tab/key) pattern used by Microsoft Learn for tab UI. Two options: (a) flatten to linear sections preserving label as section anchor, (b) emit as nested code-fenced tab block. Affects 4 of 16 frontmatter assembly failures in Power BI corpus run (developer/visuals/dynamic-drill-down.md, developer/visuals/highlight.md, etc) PLUS degrades chunk quality wherever #tab/ appears in successfully-processed files. New module: src/docline/process/docfx_tabs.py, wired into output_contract.py MD branch after include resolution. See docs/decisions/2026-06-09-powerbi-corpus-coverage.md Category A failure analysis.

023-F enhancement: cross-product absolute-path link extraction. Current cross_doc_links regex in src/docline/process/cross_doc_links.py only matches [text](*.md) patterns. Microsoft Learn extensively uses [text](/fabric/...) absolute paths for cross-product references that target sibling Microsoft docs sites. Extend regex to capture (/path/...) forms (without .md extension), emit with cross_product: true marker in the typed edge dict. Big graph-coverage win — Power BI corpus contains ~416 inline /fabric/ refs, ~hundreds more for /dax/, /azure/, /power-platform/ — currently invisible to graph layer. See docs/decisions/2026-06-09-powerbi-corpus-coverage.md cross-product references finding.
